nodes.label: DevSwarm
nodes.summary: Prompt-to-full-stack-app generator using a swarm of specialized open-weight models, review-gated by a critic and fully traced into SigNoz.

nodes.label: SigNoz
nodes.summary: Self-hosted observability platform that receives all swarm traces, metrics and logs and powers dashboards, alerts and the Swarm Doctor.

nodes.label: OpenTelemetry
nodes.summary: Instrumentation standard used across the swarm: one root span per generation, child spans per agent and per LLM call.

nodes.label: Hugging Face Inference Providers
nodes.summary: Serving layer for every open-weight model in the swarm; requires HF_TOKEN and avoids closed frontier APIs entirely.

nodes.label: Agents of SigNoz hackathon
nodes.summary: WeMakeDevs hackathon (July 20-26, 2026) DevSwarm is built for, entered on Track 1: AI & Agent Observability.

nodes.label: Swarm Doctor
nodes.summary: SRE meta-agent that consumes SigNoz data (traces, metrics, alerts) to diagnose swarm health in natural language and execute self-healing fallback promotions.

nodes.label: Testing/QA agent
nodes.summary: Planned agent that generates and runs tests against the live preview and routes failures back to the owning codegen agent; cut from the hackathon MVP.

nodes.label: Documentation agent
nodes.summary: Planned agent that generates README, setup and API docs from the plan plus the actual generated code; cut from the hackathon MVP.

nodes.label: Deployment/DevOps agent
nodes.summary: Planned agent for Dockerfiles, CI/CD and IaC that also injects OTel bootstrapping into generated apps; a fixed React+Express template stands in for it in the MVP.

nodes.label: Born-observable generated apps
nodes.summary: Every generated app ships already instrumented with OTel and gets an auto-provisioned SigNoz dashboard alongside its README.

nodes.label: otel-swarm instrumentation library
nodes.summary: Planned reusable open-source OTel library for any multi-agent LLM system, GenAI semconv-compliant, shipped with importable SigNoz dashboard JSON.

nodes.label: Transparency panel
nodes.summary: User-facing panel showing which model handled which part and what the critic flagged, backed by the same SigNoz trace data as ops views.

nodes.label: Live preview sandbox
nodes.summary: Isolated ephemeral runtime that actually runs generated apps; per-session network and filesystem isolation is a hard security requirement.

nodes.label: Sandbox isolation requirement
nodes.summary: Every generated app is treated as hostile until reviewed: sandboxes must be network- and filesystem-isolated per session as a launch requirement.

nodes.label: Qwen3.6-Plus
nodes.summary: Apache-2.0 1M-context model chosen as the planner's primary for tool-calling reliability and long-context task decomposition.

nodes.label: GLM-5.2
nodes.summary: Zhipu MoE MIT-licensed 1M-context model, primary for frontend codegen as leader of open-model agentic web-dev benchmarks.

nodes.label: Qwen3-Coder-480B
nodes.summary: Apache-2.0 1M-context coder model, primary for backend codegen (and planned testing agent) for balanced correctness on SWE-bench-style tasks.

nodes.label: DeepSeek-V4
nodes.summary: MIT-licensed thinking-mode model, primary for the critic because of its reasoning and self-verification profile.

nodes.label: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
nodes.summary: Fallback model for the planner role.

nodes.label: DeepSeek-V4-Flash
nodes.summary: Cheaper fallback model for the backend (and planned testing) roles.

nodes.label: Kimi K2.6
nodes.summary: Fallback for the frontend role (especially screenshot/Figma-to-code) and a second-opinion critic option in thinking mode.

nodes.label: Review catch rate
nodes.summary: Percentage of generations where the critic caught and fixed a real bug or security issue before user-facing output; the core value-prop metric.

nodes.label: Cost per generation
nodes.summary: Per-generation and per-role token/cost breakdown versus closed-model tools; the cheaper-than-closed-models thesis as a live dashboard.

nodes.label: Generation success rate
nodes.summary: Percentage of prompts producing a working deployable app, pre-review versus after review-gate fixes.

nodes.label: Time to working app
nodes.summary: Elapsed time from prompt to deployed working app, computed live from SigNoz traces.

nodes.label: DevSwarm PRD
nodes.summary: PRD.md: product requirements including the swarm architecture, SigNoz observability layer, hackathon strategy and success metrics.

nodes.label: 3-minute demo script
nodes.summary: Scripted continuous take: dispatch a prompt, show a critic catch, kill a model endpoint to trigger Swarm Doctor self-healing, open the born-observable app, close on the LLM Economics dashboard.
